profiles.about_me: Results-driven Civil Engineering professional with over 3 years of hands-on experience in high-rise residential and commercial construction. Adept at managing multi-stakeholder construction sites, enforcing safety standards, and optimizing resources to minimize waste. Committed to delivering projects that meet the highest standards of structural integrity and client satisfaction.

work_experience.description: Reviewed all types of project drawings, including RAJUK, Architectural, Structural and Perspective plans, identifying discrepancies and coordinating with relevant departments for timely corrections. Supervised day-to-day construction activities on-site, ensuring work is executed as per drawings, specifications, and schedule. Coordinated and instructed contractors to execute work according to the planned work program. Prepared monthly work schedules, achievement reports and monitored daily progress to maintain project timelines. Conducted on-site training for workers to enhance skills and ensure superior construction quality. Inspected material quality, enforced safety protocols and ensured the security of both construction activities and workforce. Focused on minimizing material wastage through proper handling and execution oversight. Ensured effective project merchandising and branding throughout the construction period. Maintained high standards of construction quality, ensuring timely project completion and client satisfaction.

Learned about quality control practices, safety measures and environmental considerations in civil works. Participated in meetings and site visits that deepened understanding of public infrastructure planning and implementation.

profiles.about_me: A dynamic and achievement-oriented Civil Engineering professional with over 7 years of hands-on experience in project management, site supervision, construction execution, and quality assurance. Proven ability to deliver infrastructure, commercial, and residential projects on time and within budget. Skilled in team leadership, vendor coordination, risk management, and ATS-compliant reporting. Seeking a Probationary Officer or Management Trainee role to leverage technical expertise, strategic planning, and operational excellence in a growth-oriented organization. Strong administration, organizing, and time management skills with the ability to work under pressure and handle confidential matters. Always ready to learn new technologies; a strong team player with solid analytical reasoning and problem-solving skills. Self-motivated, assertive, and possessing excellent communication skills with a positive, balanced, and professional demeanor. work_experience.description: Responsible for review of hydrological report, collection of all available hydrological, morphological and climatic data, planning and executing hydrological and navigational survey, analysis of data for local requirement of drainage opening, analysing and determining flood levels, bed scour, bank movement, review of design of all river bank/ slope protection works and preparing specification of works. The Sylhet area also experiences very intensive rainfall (the highest in Bangladesh). Therefore, the Consultant has carefully analysed the rainfall intensity records in the area to identify the hydrological requirements of the bridges and culverts. In total, There are 10 major bridges, length varying from 80 metres to 830 metres and 53 minor bridges, length varying from 22 metres to 78 metres and also 309 culverts for smooth drainage that the consultant has monitored.

projects.title: Feasibility Study with detailed design for Expansion & Modernization of Railway Training Academy (RTA)
projects.client: Ministry of Railways, Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh
projects.notes: This project involves the following but not limited to: Conducting feasibility study for modernization and expansion of Railway Training Academy, Halishahar, Chattogram. Preparing an innovative and appropriate organizational set up manual for improving the institutional competency of a Railway Training Academy (RTA). Preparation, Up gradation and Digitization of training manual, training modules for the RTA, Halishahar, Chattogram and Workshop Training Units (WTUs) at Pahartali, Dhaka, Ishwardi, Parbatipur. To identify and propose advanced ICT based education facilities, teaching materials and guidelines to make qualified instructors for newly procured Rolling Stock and railway systems i.e. Civil, Signal & Telecom, Electrical etc. Preparation of detail design and cost estimate for all infrastructures with modern facilities for Academy and all WTUs. Prepare Development Project Proposal (DPP) and Tender documents for investment project and assist in tender evaluation. Any other related issues instructed by the Project Director.

projects.title: Feasibility Study with detailed design for Expansion & Modernization of Railway Training Academy (RTA)
projects.client: Ministry of Railways, Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh
projects.notes: This project involves the following but not limited to: Conducting feasibility study for modernization and expansion of Railway Training Academy, Halishahar, Chattogram. Preparing an innovative and appropriate organizational set up manual for improving the institutional competency of a Railway Training Academy (RTA). Preparation, Up gradation and Digitization of training manual, training modules for the RTA, Halishahar, Chattogram and Workshop Training Units (WTUs) at Pahartali, Dhaka, Ishwardi, Parbatipur. To identify and propose advanced ICT based education facilities, teaching materials and guidelines to make qualified instructors for newly procured Rolling Stock and railway systems i.e. Civil, Signal & Telecom, Electrical etc. Preparation of detail design and cost estimate for all infrastructures with modern facilities for Academy and all WTUs. Prepare Development Project Proposal (DPP) and Tender documents for investment project and assist in tender evaluation. Any other related issues instructed by the Project Director.

projects.title: Awareness program/orientation and campaign through BCC/IEC on dengue, chikungunya, air & sound pollution & climate change effects on public health and medical waste management to employees of CC/Municipalities, students & other Stakeholders.
projects.client: Local Government Division, Ministry of Local Government, Rural Development & Co-operatives.
projects.notes: 1. The Local Government Division, Ministry of Local Government, Rural Development & Co-operatives has received financing from the World Bank toward the cost of the IUPHPSP and intends to apply part of the proceeds toward payments under the contract for Awareness program and campaign on (1) air and sound pollution and climate change effects on public health; (2) Consultation with different stakeholders; (3) Healthy lifestyles and Creating Consciousness about non-communicable diseases and (4) Preventive Measures from Smoking, Dust, Unnecessary use of Vehicle Horns. 2. The Local Government Division now invites sealed Bids from eligible Bidders for Awareness program and campaign on (1) air and sound pollution and climate change effects on public health; (2) Consultation with different stakeholders; (3) Healthy lifestyles and Creating Consciousness about non-communicable diseases and (4) Preventive Measures from Smoking, Dust, Unnecessary use of Vehicle Horns. 3. Bidding will be conducted through international competitive procurement using a Request for Bids (RFB) as specified in the World Bank's "Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers 1st September 2025 ("Procurement Regulations") and is open to all eligible Bidders as defined in the Procurement Regulations. 4. Bids will be evaluated in accordance with the evaluation process set out in the bidding documents. The following weightings shall apply for Rated Criteria (including techni-cal and non-price factors: 50% and for Bid cost: 50%). 5. Interested eligible Bidders may obtain further information and inspect the bidding doc-ument during office hours 0900 to 1600 hours at the address given below. For the con-venience of the Proposers an unofficial electronic copy of the RFB document can also be viewed at website https://iuphpsp.lgd.gov.bd/ (for inspection purpose only). 6. The bidding document in English may be purchased by interested eligible Bidders upon the submission of a written application to the address below and upon payment of a non-refundable fee BDT 10,000.00 or USD 100.00. The method of payment will be Cashier's Cheque or Pay Order. The document may be collected from project office or will be sent by querier. 7. Bids must be delivered to the address below on or before 16.00 hours on July 06, 2026. Electronic Bidding will not be permitted. Late Bids will be rejected. The outer Bid envelopes marked "ORIGINAL BID", and the inner envelopes marked "TECHNICAL PART" will be publicly opened in the presence of the Bidders' designated representa-tives and anyone who chooses to attend, at the address below. All envelopes marked "FINANCIAL PART" shall remain unopened and will be held in safe custody of the Employer until the second public Bid opening. 8. All Bids must be accompanied by a Bid Security shall be BDT 68,00,000.00 (sixty-eight lakh) or USD 56,000.00 (fifty-six thousand) only as mentioned in the bidding document. 9. Attention is drawn to the Procurement Regulations requiring the Borrower to disclose information on the successful bidder's beneficial ownership, as part of the Contract Award Notice, using the Beneficial Ownership Disclosure Form as included in the bid-ding document. 10. The address(es) referred to above is (are): Abul Khair Mohammad Hafizullah Khan, Joint Secretary, Local Government Division & Project Director, Improvement of Urban Public Health Preventive Services Project (IUPHPSP), DPHE Bhaban (level 8), 14 Shaheed Captain Mansur Ali Sarani, Kakrail, Dhaka-1000, Bangladesh. Phone no- +88-01711586160, email: pd.uphpp2024.lgd@gmail.com.

project.title: Capacity building of women traders on trade related regulatory regime and E-Commerce solutions and procedures in 08 Divisional HQ including Reform dialogues (Policy Dialogues) to resolve the challenges facing the women traders.
project.notes:
Invitation for   Request for Expressions of Interest   Status: Approved  GOVERNMENT OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF BANGLADESH  Ministry/Division   :   Ministry of Commerce  Agency   :   Ministry of Commerce  Procuring Entity Name   :   Bangladesh Regional Connectivity Project-1 (BRCP-1)  Procuring Entity Code   :   2230029  Procuring Entity District   :   Dhaka  Expression of Interest for Selection of   :   Consulting Firm   (National)   (Lump-Sump)  Title Of Service   :   Capacity building of women traders on trade related regulatory regime and E-Commerce solutions and procedures in 08 Divisional HQ including Reform dialogues (Policy Dialogues) to resolve the challenges facing the women traders.  EOI Ref. No.   :   26.00.0000.066.07.067.26-81  Date   :   10/06/2026  KEY INFORMATION  Procurement Sub-Method   :   Consultants Qualifications Selection (CQS)  FUNDING INFORMATION  Budget and Source of Funds   :   Development Budget Grant/Credit  Development Partners   :  PARTICULAR INFORMATION  Project/Programme Name   :  EOI Closing Date and Time   :   28/06/2026 2.00  Publication Date   :   14/06/2026  6/14/26, 11:21 PM   BPPA | Bangladesh Public Procurement Authority  https://www.bppa.gov.bd/advertisement-services/details-10619.html   1/3
INFORMATION FOR APPLICANT  Brief Description of Assignment   :   Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS) method OR Consultant's Qualification - based Selection (CQS) method  Experience, Resources and Delivery Capacity Required   :   1. The Consulting firm shall have the legal capacity to enter into the contract with minimum 10 (Ten) years of general experience out of which at least 05 (five) years of experience in training management for public and private sector; 2. The minimum specific experience as lead consultant in providing training of at least cumulatively 500 (Five Hundred) numbers of trainees completed training course over last 05 (five) years shall be required. 3. The firm should have experience in working with development projects funded by any development partner; 4. The firm should have successfully completed at least one service contract related to capacity development (designing training module/organize training program) in any sector for women entrepreneurship development in last 05(five) years; 5. The required average annual turnover of the consulting firm shall be at least of the amount of Tk. 01 (One) Crore for the last three years; and 6. A capacity statement on available training infrastructure facilities, IT equipment, logistics support, transport and office space.  Other Details (if applicable)   :  Association with foreign firms is   :   Not Applicable  Eoi Detail Information  Ref No   Phasing Of Services   Location   Start Date   Completion Date  SD-61   One phase   Dhaka   15/07/2026   30/11/2026  6/14/26, 11:21 PM   BPPA | Bangladesh Public Procurement Authority  https://www.bppa.gov.bd/advertisement-services/details-10619.html   2/3

candidate.address: Plot# 06, Avenue# 06, Sector# 17/H - 1, Uttara, Dhaka - 1230, Bangladesh
candidate.about_me: Proactive lecturer and research leader with over 12 years of experience teaching various courses in Political Science and Social Science Research Methodology at college, university, and research organizations. Led five research projects, including a Ph.D. thesis, a fellowship, a research grant, and two consultancies. Published two peer-reviewed articles, three books, and over 50 columns. Expertise in qualitative and quantitative research, focusing on rights & protection, education, violence, and policy issues. Proficient in qualitative data collection methods like FGD, KII, IDI, and Case Studies, and experienced in designing and conducting research using PRA tools. Actively involved in research and grants for disadvantaged child education and the protection of vulnerable communities.

ranked.reasoning: Dr. Khan Sarifuzzaman is the most relevant candidate, with over 12 years of experience in lecturing and social science research, directly supporting capacity building and policy dialogue aspects. His background in Political Science and Social Science Research Methodology is highly pertinent to understanding regulatory regimes and the socio-economic context of women traders, although specific E-commerce expertise is missing.
